httpError.unauthorized: Your session has expired, please sign in again
httpError.notFound: The requested resource was not found
httpError.conflict: Operation conflict, please refresh and retry
httpError.tooManyRequests: Too many requests, please try again later
httpError.serverError: The server ran into a problem, please try again later

adminUsers.noEmailsDescription: No users currently have an email set
adminUsers.clearEmailsConfirmDescription: This will set the email field to empty for {count} users. It will not affect usernames, passwords, Telegram bindings, or Emby bindings.
adminUsers.batchToggleDescription: Applies to {count} selected users. Admin accounts are protected by the backend and skipped automatically.
adminUsers.batchLockEmbyTitle: Forbid selected users from self-unbinding Emby?
adminUsers.batchLockEmbyDescription: Writes an Emby grant lock for users with Emby bound among {count} selected targets; users without Emby are skipped. Afterwards users cannot self-unbind Emby, but admins can still force-unbind. Admin accounts are protected by the backend.
adminUsers.batchDeleteDescription: Deletes {count} selected users. Admin accounts and the current admin are protected by the backend and skipped automatically.

settings.title: Settings
settings.description: Manage your account settings and preferences
settings.localeDescription: Choose the interface language. Only locale-enabled text is covered for now; future translations can continue by extending the dictionaries.
settings.localeHelp: Language preference is stored only in this browser.
settings.telegramDescription: Bind a Telegram account to receive notifications and use bot features
settings.rebindSubmitted: Rebind request submitted. Waiting for admin review.
settings.rebindApprovedHint: Admin approved your rebind request. Unbind the current Telegram first, then bind the new one.
settings.telegramRequired: Telegram binding is required by the system and cannot be removed
settings.bindRefreshHint: Refresh this page after binding to confirm the status.
settings.embyDescription: Bind an existing Emby account to use media services
settings.pendingEmbyHint: You have Emby activation entitlement but have not created an account yet. Click Continue setup to finish registration.
settings.existingEmbyHint: If you already have an account on the Emby server, bind it here. After binding, you can use that account to access media content.
settings.embyGrantLocked: This account's Emby registration entitlement comes from a registration code, invite code, or administrator grant. It cannot be unbound and registered again by self-service.
settings.embyUnbindWarning: Self-service unbinding disables the remote Emby account first, then removes the local binding. If remote disable fails, the local binding is kept.
settings.apiKeyDescription: Manage your API Key for external account-control integrations
settings.apiKeyCardDescription: Generate, enable, disable, or refresh your API Key
settings.passwordDescription: Change the system login password and bound Emby password separately.
settings.systemPasswordDescription: Change the website login password. This does not change the Emby account password.
settings.embyPasswordDescription: Only updates the password of the currently bound Emby account.
settings.embyExpiredDescription: The system account can still log in; Emby is disabled. Renew to restore line access.
settings.serverLinesDescription: Pick the lowest-latency line to connect to Emby. Click an address to copy it.
settings.bangumiDescription: Sync your watch progress with your own Bangumi access token. The system never syncs on your behalf with a global admin token.
settings.bangumiSyncDescription: When enabled, your Bangumi progress is synced automatically after you finish an anime on Emby. The token is stored only on the server and never echoed in plaintext.
settings.bangumiTokenConfiguredPlaceholder: Token configured. Leave blank to keep the current value.
settings.bangumiTokenConfigured: A Bangumi token is configured. You can enable sync directly.
settings.bangumiDisabledDescription: The administrator has not enabled Bangumi sync
settings.bangumiTokenRequiredDescription: A personal token is required before enabling BGM sync
settings.bangumiClearedDescription: Bangumi token removed and sync turned off
settings.bindCodeToastDescription: Send /bind {code} to the bot within {minutes} minutes
settings.bindCodeRateLimited: Bind code requests too frequent, please try again later
settings.remoteEmbyDisabledUnbound: Remote Emby account disabled and local binding removed
settings.remoteEmbyNotFoundUnbound: Remote account does not exist or needs no disabling; local binding removed
settings.passwordFieldsRequired: Enter both the current and new password
settings.passwordMismatch: The two new passwords do not match
settings.systemPasswordUpdatedDescription: Only the system login password was updated
settings.embyPasswordUpdatedDescription: Only the Emby password was updated
settings.embyBindDialogDescriptionCreate: Enter the Emby username and password to create
settings.embyBindDialogDescriptionBind: Enter your Emby server username and password to verify your identity
settings.embyPasswordOptionalHint: (leave blank if the account has no password)
settings.embyPasswordPlaceholder: Enter the Emby password (can be blank)
settings.embyCredentialsHint: Your Emby credentials must be verified to bind (leave blank for passwordless accounts)
settings.emailDialogDescription: Enter your email address for password recovery and important notifications
settings.systemPasswordDialogDescription: Change the system login password. The Emby password is not changed.
settings.newPasswordPlaceholder: At least 8 characters with upper, lower, and digits
settings.telegramRebindDialogDescription: After admin approval, your current Telegram is unbound so you can bind a new Telegram account.
settings.rebindReasonPlaceholder: e.g. the original Telegram account can no longer log in and needs to rebind to a new one

login.renewBeforeLogin: Please renew with a renewal code before logging in
login.title: Enter {site}
login.forgotPassword: Forgot password? Recover with your Emby account

forgotPassword.oneTimePassword: The new password is shown only once, save it now
forgotPassword.title: Recover web login password
forgotPassword.description: After verifying your bound Emby account, the system generates a new web login password.
forgotPassword.copyHint: The new password is shown only once. Copy it now and change it after logging in.

register.bindCodeGeneratedDescription: Send /bind <bind code> in a private chat with the Telegram bot to verify
register.bindCodeFailedDescription: Check API reachability and Telegram bot configuration
register.telegramBoundDescription: Click the Register button below to enter the system
register.retryBindCode: Please get a new bind code and try again
register.websocketFailed: Browser cannot establish a WebSocket connection
register.retryGetBindCode: Please get a new bind code
register.regCodeRequiredDescription: This site requires a registration code to register
register.passwordMismatchDescription: Please confirm both passwords are the same
register.telegramRequiredDescription: After getting the bind code, send /bind <bind code> in the bot's private chat
register.sendBindCommand: Send /bind {code} in the bot's private chat
register.usernameTaken: This username is taken, please choose another
register.successDescription: Log in with your system account; after login you'll be guided to set up your Emby account
register.introWithCode: Use a registration code to create a system account and gain eligibility to set up Emby later.
register.introWithoutCode: Create a system account first; after login you can redeem registration/renewal codes on the dashboard and open or renew Emby.
register.aboutRegCodeRequired: This site requires a registration code at sign-up. After successful registration, usage is recorded and Emby setup eligibility is granted.
register.aboutRegCodeOptional: This site does not require a registration code at sign-up. Register and log in first, then redeem a registration or renewal code on the dashboard.
register.createDescriptionWithCode: Fill in your account info and registration code to create a system account.
register.createDescriptionWithoutCode: Fill in the info below to create a system account. Redeem registration codes on the dashboard after login.
register.passwordPlaceholder: At least 8 chars, with upper/lowercase letters and digits
register.regCodePlaceholder: Enter the registration code issued by an admin
register.regCodeConsumptionHint: A registration code is only consumed on successful registration; invalid, expired, or used codes are not consumed.
register.openBotChat: Open the service bot's private chat in Telegram.
register.bindInstructions: After clicking Get bind code, send /bind <bind code> in the bot's private chat to verify.
register.botNotConfigured: The admin has not configured a recognizable bot account. If you can't get a bind code, contact an administrator.
register.sendCommandBelow: Send the following command in the bot's private chat:
register.waitingVerification: Waiting for bot verification… ({minutes} minutes left)
register.backToLogin: Already have an account? Back to login

errorBoundary.title: Something went wrong rendering this page
errorBoundary.description: This section failed to load. Try again, or refresh the page or go home if it keeps failing.

score.disabledDescription: When the admin disables check-in, the check-in page shows no actions.
score.missReset: Missing a day resets the streak
score.missKeep: Streak is kept even if you miss a day
score.bonusDisabledDescription: The admin disabled streak bonuses. Streak days are still recorded but no extra {currency} is granted.
score.historyEmpty: No check-in records yet, try checking in.

invite.systemClosedCreateDescription: You cannot generate new invite codes right now, but you can still create dedicated renewal codes for existing direct invitees.
invite.cannotCreate: You cannot generate invite codes right now
invite.invalidDays: Enter a valid number of days
invite.invalidRenewDays: Enter a valid number of renewal days
invite.copyManual: Copy failed, please select and copy manually
invite.disableConfirmDescription: This invite code has been used and can only be disabled, not physically deleted.
invite.deleteConfirmDescription: Unused invite codes will be permanently deleted and cannot be recovered.
invite.childWebDisabledReason: This invitee's web account is disabled but still bound to Emby.
invite.childEmbyExpiredReason: This invitee's Emby entitlement has expired.
invite.detachConfirmDescription:
{reason}

This deletes their remote Emby account, clears the local Emby binding, and detaches the invite relation. The web account's current enabled/disabled state is unchanged.
invite.systemNotOpenDescription: You cannot generate new invite codes. If you already have direct invitees, refresh and continue creating dedicated renewal codes for them.
invite.title: Invite Center
invite.description: Generate invite codes to invite new Emby users. After an invitee registers, a tree relationship is created with you.
invite.closedBanner: The invite system is closed; you cannot generate new invite codes.
invite.closedBannerDescription: Existing direct invitees can still get dedicated renewal codes. For invitees whose Emby has expired or whose web account is disabled but still bound to Emby, you can delete Emby and detach.
invite.directInviteCount: Number of people you invited directly
invite.maxGrantDays: Up to {days} days can be granted
invite.noParent: You currently have no parent and are an invite-tree root node.
invite.noChildrenWithInvite: No invitees yet. Once an invite code is used, the full child tree appears here.
invite.noChildrenClosed: No invitees yet. The invite system is closed, so new invitees cannot be added.
invite.emptyCodes: No invite codes yet. Click the button in the top right to generate your first one.
invite.createDialogDescription: Share the invite code with a friend. After they register and log in, they fill it into the code redemption area on the dashboard.
invite.daysHelp: The validity after the invitee creates an Emby account with this code. It cannot exceed your grantable limit of {days} days, and cannot be permanent.
invite.targetUsernamePlaceholder: Leave empty so any eligible user can use it
invite.targetUsernameHelp: If set, only this web username can use this invite code, compared case-insensitively. Useful for pre-assigning a code to a specific member.
invite.renewDialogDescription: This renewal code may only be used by {username}. Other accounts will be rejected.
invite.renewCodeMeta: Target: {username} · renew {days} days · code valid {hours} hours
invite.renewUidWarning: This code is bound to target UID #{uid}; do not share it with anyone else.
invite.renewDaysHelp: Up to {days} days, determined by your remaining validity.

media.selectSourceDescription: When searching by ID, choose TMDB or Bangumi
media.notFoundDescription: This ID does not exist or has been removed
media.requestSuccessDescription: The admin will handle your request soon
media.disabledDescription: When the admin disables media requests, the request center shows no actions.
media.title: Media Search
media.description: Find the titles you love, we bring them home for you
media.idSearchHint: Choose a specific source for exact matching
media.namePlaceholder: Enter a name, TMDB URL, or Bangumi URL...
media.requestsDescription: Track the status of the requests you submitted
media.inventoryAvailableDescription: If you have subtitle, source, or version issues, add a note and submit feedback
media.inventoryMissingDescription: After you submit a request, the admin will arrange the download
media.issuePlaceholder: Describe subtitle, source, quality, or version issues

dashboard.partialLoadFailed: Some dashboard data failed to load
dashboard.partialLoadFailedDescription: Failed modules: {modules}. Click refresh in the top right to retry.
dashboard.codeRequired: Enter a registration / renewal / invite code
dashboard.codeRequestExisting: A code request is already being processed
dashboard.codeRequestQueued: Code added to the processing queue
dashboard.codeStillQueued: Code is still in the queue
dashboard.refreshLater: Refresh the page later to see the result
dashboard.directRegisterDisabled: The admin has not enabled open registration
dashboard.alreadyBoundEmby: You have already bound an Emby account
dashboard.bindTelegramFirst: Complete Telegram binding in Settings → Telegram first
dashboard.embyLimitReached: The number of Emby-bound users has reached the limit ({used}/{limit})
dashboard.embyUsernameRule: 3-20 letters, digits, or underscores, not starting with a digit
dashboard.passwordRule: At least 8 characters, including upper and lower case letters and digits
dashboard.queuePositionAutoComplete: Queued (position {position}); it will complete automatically soon
dashboard.refreshToView: It will complete automatically; refresh the page to view
dashboard.refreshLatest: Refresh the page to view the latest status
dashboard.pendingRegcodeDescription: Your system account is ready but no Emby account is bound yet. Complete registration via the setup dialog.
dashboard.pendingDirectDescription: Your system account is ready but no Emby account is bound yet. Activate it from the open registration entry below.
dashboard.pendingAdminDescription: This account can log in. For media services, contact the admin to activate an Emby account.
dashboard.normalDescription: Welcome back, your account status is normal
dashboard.telegramManageHint: You can manage the binding in personal settings
dashboard.telegramRebindPending: Rebind request submitted, waiting for admin review
dashboard.telegramRequired: Telegram binding is required for full access
dashboard.telegramBindHint: Generate a bind code in personal settings to link
dashboard.embyExpiredDescription: The system account can still log in to view info, but the Emby account is disabled. It will restore line access automatically after renewal.
dashboard.codePlaceholder: Enter a registration, renewal, or invite code
dashboard.grantedEmbyTitle: You have been granted the right to register Emby
dashboard.grantedEmbyDescription: The admin cleared your registration queue and granted Emby activation. Click the button below to fill in your Emby username and password.
dashboard.directRegisterDescription: Open registration is enabled. Click the button to fill in your Emby username and password to activate.
dashboard.registerComplete: Registration complete, status kept for 15 minutes
dashboard.durationConfigured: (Configured by the admin; it applies automatically after this activation)
dashboard.codeEmbyHint: Redeeming a code only grants Emby activation rights and reserves the username; the password is set in the Emby activation dialog below.

adminViolations.title: Violation Audit
adminViolations.clearConfirmDescription: This cannot be undone and will delete all {count} violation audit records.

adminLogs.streamDisconnected: Live log connection lost, syncing via polling.
adminLogs.pollFailed: Live log polling failed. Check login status and the backend log endpoint.
adminLogs.title: Runtime Logs
adminLogs.description: Backend runtime logs, process status, and host status.

adminTelegramRebind.title: Telegram Rebind Review
adminTelegramRebind.description: Review Telegram rebind requests submitted by users
adminTelegramRebind.approveDescription: After approval, the user's current Telegram is unbound so they can bind a new Telegram account.
adminTelegramRebind.rejectDescription: After rejection, the user receives the admin's rejection result.
adminTelegramRebind.bulkSelected: This will process the {count} selected pending requests.

adminRequests.missingRequireKey: This request is missing require_key and cannot be operated on
adminRequests.title: Media Request Review

adminAnnouncements.renderPlainHint: Preserves line breaks, shown as-is, all tags displayed literally
adminAnnouncements.renderMarkdownHint: Supports headings, bold/italic, code, quotes, lists, task lists, tables, images, links, etc.
adminAnnouncements.deleteConfirmDescription: The announcement will be permanently deleted and cannot be recovered.
adminAnnouncements.title: Announcements
adminAnnouncements.description: Publish site-wide announcements with level, pinning, and expiry. Hidden announcements are not shown to end users.
adminAnnouncements.emptyHint: Click "New announcement" in the top right to publish the first one
adminAnnouncements.dialogDescription: The announcement is shown to all site users and can have a level, pinning, and expiry.
adminAnnouncements.pinDescription: Pinned announcements appear at the top of the list
adminAnnouncements.visibleDescription: If off, it is saved as a draft and not shown to users

adminStatus.title: Server Status Panel
adminStatus.description: Shows system health, runtime stats, and public info.
adminStatus.healthDescription: Check whether the backend service, database, and Emby connection are working.
adminStatus.systemArchitectureDescription: Shows the current service version, feature toggles, and runtime limits.
adminStatus.systemStatsDescription: Shows Emby, registration code, and user overview data.

adminInvite.detachDescription: This user becomes a new root node; their invitees stay under this user.
adminInvite.depthDescription: 1 means only the current user, N means the current user plus N-1 levels of invitees, 0 means the whole subtree.
adminInvite.depthInvalid: Level must be a non-negative integer
adminInvite.wholeSubtree: This applies to the whole subtree.
adminInvite.deleteWholeSubtree: This deletes the local and Emby accounts of the whole subtree.
adminInvite.deleteDepth: This deletes the local and Emby accounts within {depth} levels.
adminInvite.title: Invite Forest
adminInvite.description: View invite relations, root nodes, and invitee counts, and run cascade operations on a branch.
adminInvite.emptyDescription: Once invite codes are used, user relations appear here.
adminInvite.noMatches: No users match the current filter.

embyPending.defaultDays: Determined by the admin's default configuration
embyPending.title: Complete Emby account registration
embyPending.description: Your system account is ready but no Emby account is bound yet. Create one to use media services; if it fails, you can retry next login.
embyPending.usernameHelp: We recommend keeping it the same as your system username; if taken, choose another.
embyPending.passwordPlaceholder: At least 8 chars, with upper/lower case and digits

apiKey.title: API Key Management
apiKey.description: Create and manage API Keys for external system calls
apiKey.deleteConfirmDescription: Once deleted, this key stops working immediately and cannot be recovered.
apiKey.usageDescription: An API Key lets external systems access your account. Do not share it; it cannot be recovered after deletion.
apiKey.migrationHint: If the backend returns 500, confirm the Go backend finished database migration and restarted
apiKey.emptyDescription: Click the "New" button in the top right to create your first API Key
apiKey.copyMasked: Plaintext is no longer visible, only the masked value was copied
apiKey.plaintextOnce: Plaintext is shown once at creation. If lost, delete and recreate.
apiKey.copyImmediately: Copy and save it now; you cannot view it again after closing.
apiKey.name: Name
apiKey.readonlyOpsDescription: Read-only operations such as querying info

appearance.imageTypeError: Only JPG, PNG, GIF, and WebP images are supported
appearance.needOneBackground: At least one background is required
appearance.lightThemeDescription: Base image for daytime or light mode
appearance.darkThemeDescription: Base image for night or dark mode
appearance.previewHint: After saving, it refreshes on the home page and side area
appearance.flowDescription: When on, preset gradients flow slowly and the theme's image URL is cleared automatically.
appearance.backgroundUploadHint: Max 5MB. You still need to click Save background after uploading.
appearance.title: Appearance
appearance.description: Manage your background and avatar in one place. Saving refreshes the related areas.
appearance.saveBackgroundDescription: Preview and form changes are kept only on this page; they apply only after you save.
appearance.currentAvatarDescription: The avatar is shown in navigation, profile, and admin lists.
appearance.chooseAvatar: Click to choose an avatar image
appearance.avatarFormatHint: Supports JPG, PNG, GIF, WebP; applies immediately after upload.
appearance.avatarUsageHint: The avatar is shown across the site. To avoid cropping distortion, use a centered square image.

announcements.pageDescription: All announcements and history published by admins, sorted by pinning and publish time.

adminEmby.title: Emby Management
adminEmby.description: Manage Emby server connection, user sync, and data cleanup
adminEmby.connDesc: One-click test of the Emby server's network connectivity, API authentication, user list, and library
adminEmby.partialTestsFailed: Some tests failed. Check the Emby configuration.
adminEmby.botDesc: Send a test message to all configured groups and channels to verify the bot works
adminEmby.partialTargetsFailed: Some targets failed. Check the group/channel configuration.
adminEmby.botTestSentDesc: Sent a test message to {count} targets
adminEmby.botTestCheckConfig: Check whether the group/channel configuration is correct
adminEmby.userMgmtDesc: Fetch the user list from Emby, compare with the local database, and import, sync, or clean up
adminEmby.searchPlaceholder: Search Emby name / EmbyID / local username / UID / Telegram ID
adminEmby.orphansHint: (Local EMBYID exists but no longer present on Emby)
adminEmby.usersNotAutoLoaded: The Emby user list is not loaded automatically. Click "Fetch data" to load it when you need to compare or clean up.
adminEmby.cleanupCardDesc: Clean up stale data to ease testing and keep the database tidy
adminEmby.cleanOrphansDesc: Remove EMBYIDs in the local database that point to Emby users that no longer exist. Local users are not deleted.
adminEmby.resetBindingsDesc: Clear every user's EMBYID binding without deleting Emby-side accounts. Suitable for resetting test environments.
adminEmby.resetDialogDesc: This clears the EMBYID bindings of all local users. It is a dangerous operation usually used only in test environments. It is irreversible; you must re-sync to restore bindings.

adminDatabase.title: Database Backup
adminDatabase.description: Snapshot backup, inspection, restore, and deletion are enabled by default. Migration appears only after it is explicitly enabled in the config file.
adminDatabase.migrationOffTitle: Database migration is disabled by default
adminDatabase.migrationOffDesc: To migrate, set migration_panel_enabled = true under [Database] in config.toml and save. Backup, inspection, and restore are unaffected.
adminDatabase.postgresNotReadyDesc: Before choosing PostgreSQL as the target, fill in the PostgreSQL connection in Configuration and save. Preflight validates the connection; execution creates a protective backup automatically.
adminDatabase.backupMgmtDesc: Create, inspect, restore, and delete database state snapshots. A protective backup is created before restoring.
adminDatabase.notePlaceholder: Note: e.g. before upgrade, before migration, after manual check
adminDatabase.sourceDesc: Choose the data source to read from.
adminDatabase.currentGoStateDesc: Read the snapshot of the running JSON or PostgreSQL state.
adminDatabase.targetDesc: Choose the write target; execution only writes the target and does not switch the running backend.
adminDatabase.pgTargetDesc: Write the snapshot into the PostgreSQL twilight_state JSONB table.
adminDatabase.pgPreflightHint: Preflight connects to PostgreSQL and, if the target database is missing and permissions allow, creates it and prepares the state table.
adminDatabase.jsonTargetDesc: Write the source data back to the Twilight JSON state file, suitable for rollback or offline verification.
adminDatabase.safetyDesc: Confirm backup, mapping, and target state.
adminDatabase.protectiveBackupHint: Before migration, the backend first backs up the running state; the SQLite data source is disabled.
adminDatabase.migrationSourceHint: Preflight reads only the running JSON or PostgreSQL snapshot and no longer reads SQLite files.
adminDatabase.targetStateHint: Connection and target path appear after preflight.
adminDatabase.databaseCreatedHint: This preflight automatically created the PostgreSQL target database.
adminDatabase.bottomPreviewDesc: Preflight only reads the source and checks the target; it does not write to the target database.
adminDatabase.previewEmptyHint: Choose source and target, then click Generate preview to see migration size, mapping, backup policy, and warnings.
adminDatabase.confirmMigrateDesc: Preflight is complete. After confirmation, a protective backup is created first, then the snapshot is written to the target backend.
adminDatabase.writeTargetOnlyDesc: To switch the running backend, make sure database.driver is set to the target type and restart the service.
adminDatabase.backupDetailDesc: View backup size, creation time, and snapshot data volume.
adminDatabase.confirmRestoreDesc: Restore overwrites the current database state; a protective backup is created before execution.
adminDatabase.highRiskDesc: Confirm the current database is no longer needed, or that the protective backup can be used to roll back.
adminDatabase.protectiveBackupCreated: A protective backup was created before restoring
adminDatabase.deleteBackupDesc: Delete database backup {name}? This cannot be undone.
adminDatabase.migrateCompleteWritten: {users} users written to the target backend

adminConfig.saveSuccessDescription: Configuration was hot-reloaded. Scheduler jobs will refresh automatically.
adminConfig.serverIconImmediateSaveWarning: Uploading the server icon saves server_icon immediately. Save or reset current configuration changes first.
adminConfig.serverIconUploadSuccessDescription: Server icon was saved and hot-reloaded.
adminConfig.serverIconUploadFailureDescription: Check the image format and network connection
adminConfig.deleteBackupDescription: Delete configuration backup {name}? This cannot be undone.
adminConfig.updateCompleteDescription: Code has been updated. Services will restart according to the settings.
adminConfig.pageDescription: View and edit project configuration with visual and source-file editors
adminConfig.serverIconDescription: Keep using an HTTPS URL or server path, or upload an image directly as an administrator.
adminConfig.pendingChangesDescription: The current configuration will be backed up before saving, then applied after backend hot reload succeeds.
adminConfig.remainingChanges: {count} more changes are not expanded in the summary. Review the full list in the save confirmation dialog.
adminConfig.tomlDescription: Edit the TOML configuration file directly. It reloads automatically after saving.
adminConfig.tomlAutoBackup: A config.toml.backup file is created automatically when saving
adminConfig.tomlUnsavedAlert: Unsaved changes detected. Click Save to apply them.
adminConfig.backupsDescription: A protective backup is created before saving. You can also create, view, restore, or delete backups manually.
adminConfig.safetyMechanismDescription: Restore validates TOML first, creates a protective backup, and rolls back the file if hot reload fails.
adminConfig.backupListDescription: Backup files are stored in the config subdirectory under the database backup directory.
adminConfig.gitUpdateDescription: Pull a branch from the specified repository and optionally restart systemd services. Use trusted repositories only.
adminConfig.productionOperationDescription: The backend runs git pull --ff-only and restarts twilight / twilight-bot / twilight-scheduler. If the server has uncommitted code changes, the update fails without overwriting them.
adminConfig.autoRestartDescription: systemctl restart for three Twilight services
adminConfig.restoreDialogDescription: Restore replaces config.toml and immediately attempts hot reload. It rolls back automatically on failure.
adminConfig.restoreHighRiskDescription: After confirmation, the current configuration is backed up, then replaced with the selected backup content.
adminConfig.saveDialogDescription: The following configuration items will be updated. The original file will be backed up before saving.

adminScheduler.cleanupNoEmbyDescription: Web accounts registered beyond this many days, without Emby and without an Emby entitlement, are cleaned up.
adminScheduler.cleanupPendingDescription: Each run revokes all unused Emby entitlements without deleting Web accounts.
adminScheduler.enforceGroupDescription: When a user rejoins the group and the account has not expired, whether to auto-unban and enable their Emby and system account. When off, only marks for review. Note: auto-unban does not apply when ban_on_leave is on.
adminScheduler.manualModeHint: After saving, the scheduler will not run this job automatically. Admins can still trigger it via Run now.
adminScheduler.editorFootnote: Changes take effect immediately and persist across restarts. Choose Disable auto to fully stop automatic scheduling; click Reset to default to clear the override.
adminScheduler.autoUnbanHint: When on, if a scan finds the user has rejoined and the account has not expired, their system account and Emby access are restored automatically. When off, they are only marked for review and an admin must enable them manually.
adminScheduler.enableAutoCleanupHint: This switch is saved to the scheduler's database params. When off, scheduled auto runs are skipped, but manual runs can still force execution.
adminScheduler.resetTitleCustom: Clear customization and restore config.toml default
adminScheduler.title: Scheduler
adminScheduler.description: Manage background inspection, cleanup, and sync jobs. Jobs run asynchronously and the page polls while running; manual triggers do not block the page.
adminScheduler.noTimedJobsWarning: No registered automatic jobs found. Make sure the scheduler process is running, otherwise jobs only run when you click Run now.
adminScheduler.jobListHint: Filter quickly by run status; action buttons are at the bottom of each job card.
adminScheduler.noRunRecord: This job has no run records yet.
adminScheduler.rejoinDetected: Found {count} disabled users who rejoined the group. Re-verify and enable them in bulk.
adminScheduler.triggeredDescription: The job runs in the background; check status on its card
adminScheduler.manualTriggerDescription: Applies only to this manual run and does not change config.toml defaults.
adminScheduler.preserveTgHint: When checked, Web accounts bound to Telegram but without Emby are kept.
adminScheduler.ignoreEnabledHint: When checked, you can run once manually even if auto cleanup is disabled in config.
adminScheduler.dryRunHint: When checked, only counts kick targets without actually kicking. See the summary for results.
adminScheduler.maxPerRunHint: Avoids Telegram rate limits; default 200, max 500.
adminScheduler.kickWarning: ⚠️ Using the group roster as the baseline, cross-checks the users table and kicks members with no system account / disabled / no Emby.

adminRegcodes.pageSubtitle: Manage registration, renewal, whitelist and invite codes
adminRegcodes.createDesc: Choose a code type and configure parameters
adminRegcodes.typeValueHint: Type values: register=1, renew=2, whitelist=3; -1 days means permanent, 0 is treated as 30 days.
adminRegcodes.daysHelpWhitelist: Whitelist user validity; -1 is permanent, 0 is treated as 30 days
adminRegcodes.daysHelpAccount: Days added to the account after using this code; -1 is permanent, 0 is treated as 30 days
adminRegcodes.validityHelp: The code expires if unused within this period; -1 means permanently valid
adminRegcodes.useCountLimitHelp: Total number of times this code can be used; -1 means unlimited
adminRegcodes.targetUsernamePlaceholder: Leave empty for no restriction; only this Web username can use it
adminRegcodes.targetUsernameHelp: Pick one of this, TG username, or TG ID; target username matching is case-insensitive.
adminRegcodes.targetTgUsernameHelp: The Telegram username bound at registration or redemption must match.
adminRegcodes.targetTgIdHelp: The most stable targeting method; registration requires completing Telegram bind-code verification first.
adminRegcodes.decoyHelp: When used, the security action in [SAR].regcode_decoy_action is triggered.
adminRegcodes.formatHintRandom: {r}: the random part generated by the selected algorithm.
adminRegcodes.formatHintIndex: {idx}: sequence number within this batch, starting from 1.
adminRegcodes.formatHintValidityLimit: {v}: code validity in hours; {lim}: usage count limit.
adminRegcodes.formatHintFallback: Leave empty to use the default format from configuration. When {r} is absent, the backend appends a random part automatically.
adminRegcodes.selectionSummary: {selected} selected; exports all {total} on the current page when none selected.
adminRegcodes.searchRegcodePlaceholder: Search code / note / used UID / target TG
adminRegcodes.localUserGone: Local user for UID {uid} no longer exists
adminRegcodes.tgOnlyHint: No local user is currently bound to this Telegram ID.
adminRegcodes.targetConflictDesc: Fill in only one of username, TG username, or TG ID
adminRegcodes.tgIdMustBePositive: TG ID must be a positive integer
adminRegcodes.deleteCodeDesc: Code {code} will be deleted immediately; codes with usage records are also removed from the list and cannot be recovered.
adminRegcodes.codeNotFound: Code does not exist or was already deleted
adminRegcodes.selectToDelete: Select registration codes to delete first
adminRegcodes.batchDeleteDesc:
{preview}{more}

Selected codes are deleted directly; codes with usage records are also removed from the list and cannot be recovered.
adminRegcodes.clearUsageDesc: This clears all usage records for code {code} (usage count, user UIDs, Telegram IDs) and restores it to available. Registered user accounts are unaffected.
adminRegcodes.type1Desc: Used for new users to register a system account and grants the days for first-time Emby account creation. Previously generated codes still apply per the type, days, and usage count stored in the database.
adminRegcodes.type1Defaults: Default: 30-day account, permanently valid code, 1 use.
adminRegcodes.type2Desc: Usable only by users with Emby bound, to extend account validity; permanent days set the expiry to permanent.
adminRegcodes.type2Defaults: Default: +30 days, code valid for 72 hours, 1 use.
adminRegcodes.type3Desc: Upgrades the user to whitelist with permanent validity; if Emby is not bound, an Emby username and password are required to complete creation.
adminRegcodes.type3Defaults: Default: permanent account, permanently valid code, unlimited uses.
adminRegcodes.algoDescDefault: Uses the default random algorithm from configuration.
adminRegcodes.algoDesc_base32-20: 20-char easy-to-copy uppercase Base32-style without confusing characters; recommended default.
adminRegcodes.algoDesc_base32-24: 24-char easy-to-copy uppercase Base32-style, higher strength, good for public distribution.
adminRegcodes.algoDesc_base32-32: 32-char easy-to-copy uppercase Base32-style, good for high-value batch invite codes.
adminRegcodes.algoDesc_hex32: 32-char hex string, ~128-bit strength, good for import/export between systems.
adminRegcodes.algoDesc_hex40: 40-char hex string, longer, good for machine processing.
adminRegcodes.algoDesc_hex20: 20-char hex string, legacy default format, kept for compatibility.
adminRegcodes.algoDesc_base32-16: 16-char uppercase Base32-style without confusing characters, good for manual copying.
adminRegcodes.algoDesc_alnum-24: 24-char uppercase letters + digits, higher strength, balances readability and randomness.
adminRegcodes.algoDesc_alnum-16: 16-char uppercase letters + digits, balances readability and randomness.
adminRegcodes.algoDesc_alnum-32: 32-char uppercase letters + digits, good for high-strength codes.
adminRegcodes.algoDesc_urlsafe-24: 24-char URL-safe characters: upper/lower letters, digits, - and _.
adminRegcodes.algoDesc_urlsafe-32: 32-char URL-safe characters, good for passing to external systems.
adminRegcodes.algoDesc_digits-16: 16 digits, safer than 12 but still weaker than alphanumeric.
adminRegcodes.algoDesc_digits-12: 12 digits, easy to convey verbally but weaker than alphanumeric.
adminRegcodes.algoDesc_symbols-16: 16 mixed characters including symbols, good for copy-paste scenarios.
adminRegcodes.algoDesc_symbols-24: 24 mixed characters including symbols, higher randomness.
adminRegcodes.algoDesc_uuid: Standard UUID v4, long and highly unique, good for import/export between systems.
adminRegcodes.algoDesc_legacy-sha1: Legacy truncated SHA1 format, for compatibility with historical code styles.

validators.usernameFormat: Invalid username: only letters / digits / underscores are allowed, and it cannot start with a digit
validators.codeRequired: Please enter a registration / invite code
validators.codeFormat: Code may only contain letters / digits / underscores / hyphens

password.tooWeak: {label} is too weak: at least 8 characters including upper- and lower-case letters and a number
password.needLower: {label} is too weak: include at least one lowercase letter
password.needUpper: {label} is too weak: include at least one uppercase letter
password.needDigit: {label} is too weak: include at least one number
